# PRD-155: Manifest type generation

> Epic: [Contract packages](../../epics/00-contract-packages.md)

## Overview

Auto-generate two artifacts per contract package:

1. **`<Pillar>Contract`** — a structured TypeScript type union of every public surface (types, schemas, router, errors, search, ai, uri, settings). The single-source-of-truth manifest type that consumers + the registry can navigate.
2. **`<Pillar>ManifestPayload`** — the JSON-serialisable wire shape the pillar POSTs to the registry on boot. The runtime projection of the contract that contains only what the registry needs to know (no Zod schema runtime payloads, no TypeScript types — just metadata).

Both live in `@pops/<pillar>-contract/manifest`. The contract owns the wire format; the SDK (Epic 01) consumes it.

## Data Model

### Generated file: `src/manifest.generated.ts`

Committed to git (drift-checked in CI). Regenerated by `pnpm -F @pops/<pillar>-contract generate:manifest` which runs as part of `pnpm build`.

```ts
// src/manifest.generated.ts (auto-generated; do not edit)
//
// regenerated by `pnpm -F @pops/<pillar>-contract generate:manifest`
// inputs:
//   - src/types/index.ts (entity exports)
//   - src/schemas/index.ts (Zod schema exports)
//   - src/router.ts (FinanceRouter type)
//   - src/errors.ts (error discriminant exports)
//   - src/search.ts (declared search adapters)
//   - src/ai.ts (declared AI tools)
//   - src/uri.ts (declared URI types)
//   - src/settings.ts (declared settings keys)
//   - package.json (version field)

import type * as types from './types';
import type * as schemas from './schemas';
import type { FinanceRouter } from './router';
import type * as errors from './errors';

export type FinanceContract = {
  pillar: 'finance';
  version: '1.4.2'; // literal from package.json
  types: {
    Entity: types.Entity;
    Transaction: types.Transaction;
    Budget: types.Budget;
    Wishlist: types.Wishlist;
    // ...auto-emitted from src/types/index.ts
  };
  schemas: {
    EntitySchema: typeof schemas.EntitySchema;
    TransactionSchema: typeof schemas.TransactionSchema;
    // ...auto-emitted from src/schemas/index.ts
  };
  router: FinanceRouter;
  errors: {
    BudgetExceededError: errors.BudgetExceededError;
    EntityNotFoundError: errors.EntityNotFoundError;
    // ...auto-emitted from src/errors.ts
  };
  search: {
    adapters: readonly ['entities', 'transactions', 'budgets', 'wishlist'];
  };
  ai: {
    tools: readonly ['suggestTags', 'categorize', 'reviseTransaction'];
  };
  uri: {
    types: readonly [
      'finance/transaction',
      'finance/budget',
      'finance/wish-list',
      'finance/entity',
    ];
  };
  settings: {
    keys: readonly ['finance.defaultBudgetPeriod', 'finance.tagSeparator'];
  };
};

export type FinanceManifestPayload = {
  pillar: 'finance';
  version: '1.4.2';
  contract: {
    package: '@pops/finance-contract';
    version: '1.4.2';
    tag: 'contract-finance@v1.4.2';
  };
  routes: {
    queries: readonly string[]; // ['finance.transactions.list', 'finance.budgets.get', ...]
    mutations: readonly string[]; // ['finance.transactions.create', ...]
    subscriptions: readonly string[]; // (empty today; reserved)
  };
  search: { adapters: readonly string[] };
  ai: {
    tools: readonly { name: string; description: string; parameters: object }[];
  };
  uri: { types: readonly string[] };
  settings: { keys: readonly string[] };
  healthcheck: { path: '/health' };
  // `baseUrl` populated at runtime by the SDK; not part of the static payload.
};
```

### Source files the codegen reads

```
packages/<pillar>-contract/src/
├── types/index.ts          # barrel — `export * from './<entity>'`
├── schemas/index.ts        # barrel — `export * from './<entity>'`
├── router.ts               # exports the tRPC router type
├── errors.ts               # exports error classes / types
├── search.ts               # `export const searchAdapters = [...] as const`
├── ai.ts                   # `export const aiTools = [...] as const`
├── uri.ts                  # `export const uriTypes = [...] as const`
└── settings.ts             # `export const settingsKeys = [...] as const`
```

The codegen walks each, extracts the exported symbols (TS Compiler API), and emits the typed manifest.

### Helper: `buildManifestPayload()`

```ts
// src/manifest.ts (hand-written; thin runtime helper)
import type { FinanceManifestPayload } from './manifest.generated';
import { searchAdapters } from './search';
import { aiTools } from './ai';
import { uriTypes } from './uri';
import { settingsKeys } from './settings';
import pkg from '../package.json' assert { type: 'json' };

export function buildManifestPayload(args: {
  routes: FinanceManifestPayload['routes'];
}): FinanceManifestPayload {
  return {
    pillar: 'finance',
    version: pkg.version as FinanceManifestPayload['version'],
    contract: {
      package: '@pops/finance-contract',
      version: pkg.version,
      tag: `contract-finance@v${pkg.version}`,
    },
    routes: args.routes,
    search: { adapters: searchAdapters },
    ai: {
      tools: aiTools.map((t) => ({
        name: t.name,
        description: t.description,
        parameters: t.parameters,
      })),
    },
    uri: { types: uriTypes },
    settings: { keys: settingsKeys },
    healthcheck: { path: '/health' },
  };
}
```

The pillar's SDK calls this with the routes it's actually mounted, then POSTs the result to the registry.

## API Surface

### Per-contract scripts (added to `package.json`)

```jsonc
{
  "scripts": {
    "generate:manifest": "tsx scripts/generate-manifest.ts",
    "verify:manifest": "tsx scripts/verify-manifest.ts",
    "build": "tsc -b && tsx scripts/generate-openapi.ts && pnpm verify:manifest",
  },
}
```

### Shared tooling (lives in `scripts/contract/`)

- `scripts/contract/generate-manifest.ts` — for one contract package, walks the source files, emits `src/manifest.generated.ts`. Idempotent.
- `scripts/contract/verify-manifest.ts` — regenerates in-memory; diffs against committed `manifest.generated.ts`. Exits non-zero on drift.
- `scripts/contract/parse-as-const-list.ts` — shared TS-AST helper for extracting `export const X = [...] as const` arrays from `search.ts`, `ai.ts`, etc.

### Exports

```ts
// @pops/<pillar>-contract/manifest
export type { <Pillar>Contract, <Pillar>ManifestPayload } from './manifest.generated';
export { buildManifestPayload } from './manifest';
```

## Business Rules

- **The generated file is committed.** Treated like the api-extractor snapshot in PRD-154 — committed for review and drift-checked.
- **Codegen is idempotent.** Re-running with no source changes produces a byte-identical file. The script normalises ordering (alphabetical for object members) so non-significant changes don't show up in diffs.
- **Drift fails CI.** The build script runs `verify:manifest` after `generate:manifest`; if the committed file differs from the generation output, the build fails with "run `pnpm -F @pops/<pillar>-contract generate:manifest` and commit the result."
- **The version field is a literal type.** `version: '1.4.2'` not `version: string`. This makes the registry's compatibility check (Epic 02's concern) type-checkable at the call site: "I was built against `contract-finance@v1.4.2`; the registry reports `1.4.2` is deployed; types match."
- **Codegen reads only declared exports.** Anything in `src/types/internal/` (or any private file) is excluded. Convention: anything not re-exported from `src/types/index.ts` is private.
- **The runtime payload (`<Pillar>ManifestPayload`) is the wire format.** It's the structure consumers see when calling `pillar('finance').manifest()` (or whatever the registry-snapshot client surface looks like in Epic 02). Stable; semver-disciplined by PRD-154.
- **The `as const` arrays in `search.ts`, `ai.ts`, `uri.ts`, `settings.ts` are the contract author's only manual maintenance burden.** Adding a new search adapter = add a string to the array. Codegen does the rest.
- **TypeScript compiler API drives extraction.** The codegen script uses `ts.createProgram` to load the contract's source tree, then walks exported symbols. More robust than regex or AST-only approaches; handles renames, aliases, and re-exports cleanly.
- **The `buildManifestPayload()` helper stays hand-written.** Only the type metadata is auto-generated; the runtime constructor that assembles an actual payload value is small enough to maintain manually and benefits from being readable.

## Edge Cases

| Case                                                                                                      | Behaviour                                                                                                                                                                |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Contract author adds a new entity to `src/types/` but forgets to re-export from the barrel                | Codegen doesn't see it; manifest omits it; calling site type-errors because the contract doesn't advertise the type. Author adds the barrel export.                      |
| Two entities have the same name across `types/` and `schemas/` (e.g. `Transaction` + `TransactionSchema`) | Schema convention requires `Schema` suffix; codegen verifies the naming and emits `types.Transaction` + `schemas.TransactionSchema`. No collision.                       |
| Contract uses generic types (`Result<T>`, `Paginated<T>`)                                                 | Generics are emitted as-is. Consumers reference `FinanceContract['types']['PaginatedTransactions']` (the concrete instantiation) rather than the generic.                |
| Codegen encounters circular imports between source files                                                  | TS compiler resolves them. Output is unaffected.                                                                                                                         |
| `package.json` version is a prerelease (`1.4.2-beta.1`)                                                   | Literal type emitted as `'1.4.2-beta.1'`. The tag field becomes `contract-finance@v1.4.2-beta.1`. Treated as a valid version string by PRD-154's semver classifier.      |
| Codegen takes too long (>3s for the largest contract)                                                     | Cache the parsed TS program between runs; only re-parse changed source files. Typical run target: <1s.                                                                   |
| Generated file is hand-edited                                                                             | Drift check catches it; CI fails with the regenerate instruction. Re-running codegen overwrites the hand edits.                                                          |
| Contract's tRPC router file is a type-only `.d.ts` (no runtime)                                           | Codegen reads it the same way; type extraction works on any TypeScript source.                                                                                           |
| `as const` array in `search.ts` is built dynamically (`const adapters = [...x, 'new'] as const`)          | Codegen reports "search.ts must export a literal array; dynamic construction not supported" and exits non-zero.                                                          |
| Contract author wants to mark an entity as `@deprecated`                                                  | JSDoc `@deprecated` flows through the generated type. Consumers see the deprecation in their IDE. PRD-154's semver check doesn't treat deprecation as a breaking change. |

## User Stories

| #   | Story                                                                         | Summary                                                                                                                                                                   | Parallelisable                   |
| --- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| 01  | [us-01-generate-manifest-script](us-01-generate-manifest-script.md)           | `scripts/contract/generate-manifest.ts` — reads source files, emits `manifest.generated.ts`. Uses TS Compiler API.                                                        | blocked by PRD-153 us-02 + us-03 |
| 02  | [us-02-as-const-list-parser](us-02-as-const-list-parser.md)                   | `scripts/contract/parse-as-const-list.ts` — shared helper for extracting `as const` array exports                                                                         | yes — independent                |
| 03  | [us-03-build-manifest-payload-helper](us-03-build-manifest-payload-helper.md) | The hand-written `buildManifestPayload()` runtime helper + tests                                                                                                          | blocked by us-01                 |
| 04  | [us-04-verify-manifest-drift-check](us-04-verify-manifest-drift-check.md)     | `scripts/contract/verify-manifest.ts` — drift check for CI integration                                                                                                    | blocked by us-01                 |
| 05  | [us-05-build-pipeline-integration](us-05-build-pipeline-integration.md)       | Wire `generate:manifest` + `verify:manifest` into the contract package's `pnpm build`                                                                                     | blocked by us-01 + us-04         |
| 06  | [us-06-finance-pilot](us-06-finance-pilot.md)                                 | Generate finance's manifest; populate `search.ts`, `ai.ts`, `uri.ts`, `settings.ts`; verify roundtrip with a synthetic SDK boot call                                      | blocked by us-05 + PRD-153 us-07 |
| 07  | [us-07-author-documentation](us-07-author-documentation.md)                   | `packages/<pillar>-contract/README.md` template explaining how to add an entity, search adapter, AI tool, URI type, settings key (and that the manifest auto-regenerates) | yes — independent                |

## Out of Scope

- The registry itself (storing + serving manifests) — Epic 02 / PRDs 161-164.
- The SDK's runtime registration flow (taking a `ManifestPayload` and POSTing to the registry) — Epic 01 / PRD-158.
- Runtime validation of the manifest payload at the registry boundary — Epic 02 / PRD-161 (registry validates incoming payloads against a Zod schema derived from the contract).
- Cross-contract manifest aggregation ("show me all pillars that own a `Transaction`-shaped entity") — possibly Epic 06 territory if it becomes useful for search; not Theme 13.
- Manifest versioning beyond the contract's semver. The manifest is just the contract's structural snapshot; if the contract is `1.4.2`, the manifest is also at `1.4.2`. No separate manifest version.
- Manifest schema migrations. Same answer — the manifest IS the contract; migrating it = migrating the contract.
- Manifest serialisation in a wire format other than JSON (Protobuf, MessagePack). JSON is fine for the registry; no need to optimise.
